# Issue #8790: Replace deprecated `cuda.cudart` with `cuda.bindings.runtime`.
**Repository:** Project-MONAI/MONAI
**Author:** ytl0623
**Status:** closed
**Created At:** 2026-03-24T02:46:42Z

## Description
Fixes #8789 

### Description

Replaces the deprecated `cuda.cudart` import in `trt_compiler.py` with `cuda.bindings.runtime`, which is the current API introduced in `cuda-bindings` >= 12.6 (pulled in by PyTorch 2.10+).

### Types of changes
<!--- Put an `x` in all the boxes that apply, and remove the not applicable items -->
- [x] Non-breaking change (fix or new feature that would not break existing functionality).
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or new feature that would cause existing functionality to change).
- [ ] New tests added to cover the changes.
- [ ] Integration tests passed locally by running `./runtests.sh -f -u --net --coverage`.
- [ ] Quick tests passed locally by running `./runtests.sh --quick --unittests  --disttests`.
- [ ] In-line docstrings updated.
- [ ] Documentation updated, tested `make html` command in the `docs/` folder.

## Comments
### Comment by coderabbitai[bot] at 2026-03-24T02:51:43Z
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: summarize by coderabbit.ai -->
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

The patch updates monai/networks/trt_compiler.py: `cuassert` now treats CUDA call results as a tuple where the first element is a `cudaError_t`-like object and checks `err.value != 0` for failure. On failure it attempts to enrich the raised RuntimeError by calling `cudart.cudaGetErrorName(err)` and `cudart.cudaGetErrorString(err)` and falls back to a basic `CUDA ERROR {err.value}` message while logging a debug note if enrichment fails. The `cuassert` docstring and `Raises` section were expanded to reflect the tuple structure and RuntimeError behavior.

## Estimated code review effort

🎯 2 (Simple) | ⏱️ ~12 minutes

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

### Comment by ytl0623 at 2026-03-25T06:47:30Z
Hi @ericspod,
Sorry to bother you. The CI failures are pre-existing infrastructure issues unrelated to this PR — `backports.tarfile` is missing in the Python 3.10 runner environment.
### Comment by ytl0623 at 2026-03-25T07:12:05Z
> Hi @ericspod, Sorry to bother you. The CI failures are pre-existing infrastructure issues unrelated to this PR — `backports.tarfile` is missing in the Python 3.10 runner environment.

I pulled a [request](https://github.com/Project-MONAI/MONAI/pull/8792) to try to resolve this issue.
### Comment by ericspod at 2026-03-27T16:33:44Z
Hi @ytl0623 I see that this PR now only updates the error function and not the cudart import, is that correct? I think it's fine if so, we'll just run tests again after other merges, such as the fix for the missing dependency you submitted.
### Comment by ytl0623 at 2026-03-28T14:52:45Z
Hi @ericspod, yesss, that's it.  I'd prefer to leave the test coverage for a follow-up PR rather than this one. Thanks!
